creator: khera@kcilink.com
text:
>>>>> "EK" == Emiel Kollof <coolvibe@hackerheaven.org> writes:

>> make in kdebase2 fails to find X libs if /usr/X11R6/bin is not in the path.
>> my root environment doesn't have X in the path.

EK> Uh? /usr/X11R6/_bin_? No, you must be thinking of /usr/X11R6/lib. Did
EK> you check /etc/ld.so.conf? what does $LD_LIBRARY_PATH say? I can build X
EK> stuff fine from root in the ports and I'm using the standard tcsh

No, I'm not misspoken.  If /usr/X11R6/bin is not in $PATH the configure
fails.  It seems that the kde configure script uses the $PATH to guess
at the proper library locations.

try it without and with /usr/X11R6/bin in your $PATH.  you'll see.
